rationale.root_cause: They identified the unsafe copy at program.c:9 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained that a user-controlled argv string can exceed buf[256], causing a stack-buffer-overflow. This localizes the defect to the correct line and names the overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed strncpy with explicit null-termination, but did not reject overlong input before copying. The rubric requires rejecting oversize input in addition to bounding and null-terminating.
rationale.global_fix: They advised using bounded string APIs (strncpy/strlcpy/snprintf) or dynamic allocation across the codebase, removing raw unbounded string handling from the data path. This satisfies the root-cause remediation criterion.
